content: src/triggers/ module layout (R-06 scheduler) mirrors src/warren-config/: errors.ts, config.ts (WARREN_SCHEDULER_TICK_MS/_DISABLED/WARREN_SD_BINARY env vars), schema.ts (sd list envelope zod), cron.ts (croner facade), repo.ts (re-export of db/repos/triggers), dispatch.ts (dispatchCronTrigger + dispatchScheduledSeed), seeds-extension.ts (sd list/update shell-outs), tick.ts (runTick + startScheduler), index.ts.

title: No-catch-up cron semantics via previousRun > lastFiredAt
rationale: A 4-hour outage on an hourly cron should fire once on warren restart, not 4 stacked runs. dispatchCronTrigger fires iff parsedCron.previousRun(now) > lastFiredAt; after firing it stamps lastFiredAt=now (not prev) so any other missed slots are silently skipped.

content: First observation of a cron trigger seeds lastFiredAt=now and nextFireAt=parsedCron.nextRun(now) but does NOT fire — the prev-vs-last comparison would otherwise dispatch all historical slots on the first tick. Returns {kind:'seeded'}; the subsequent tick is the first eligible to fire.

cron.ts (croner facade) probes the parsed pattern by calling job.nextRun(new Date(0)) inside parseCron — croner defers timezone validation until the first nextRun call, so without the eager probe an invalid IANA zone surfaces as a runtime throw inside the dispatcher rather than {ok: false} at parse time. Both previousRun and nextRun wrap their calls in try/catch returning null on failure so the dispatcher's prev-vs-last comparison stays type-safe.

Scheduled-seed dispatch write-order (pl-2f15 risk #4): spawn run FIRST → if fired, attempt clearScheduledFor SECOND; if clearScheduledFor fails, emit a trigger.cleared_extension_failed event (stream='system') on the dispatched run via events.append at maxSeqForRun+1. The seed's scheduledFor extension stays set, so the next tick will re-fire UNLESS the operator clears it manually — but the lingering scheduledFor + the system event give the operator the diagnostic surface.

Scheduler single-flight (pl-2f15 risk #5): startScheduler wraps the tick callback in an inFlight guard — a tick still running when the interval fires logs scheduler.tick_skipped and the next interval picks up against fresh state. runOnce() respects the same guard so test-driven ticks behave identically. handle.stop() flips a 'stopped' flag, clears the interval, and awaits any in-flight tick to drain.

Scheduler env contract: WARREN_SCHEDULER_TICK_MS (positive int, default 60000), WARREN_SCHEDULER_DISABLED (truthy set {1,true,yes,on,case-insensitive,whitespace-tolerant}; default false), WARREN_SD_BINARY (default 'sd', empty string rejects). Subminute ticks are reachable via the env var but cron grammar is minute-grained so it just wastes work; tests inject the tickMs directly to bypass the floor.

Seeds CLI shell-out shape: 'sd list --format json' returns {success?: bool, issues: [{id, status, title?, extensions?}]} — passthrough is OK, schema only requires id+status+extensions for filtering. 'sd update <id> --extensions <json>' merges shallowly; warren passes {scheduledFor: null, lastScheduledRun: <runId>} to move the field. Spawn cwd is the project clone root; seeds resolves .seeds/ relative to cwd. Failures (non-zero exit or non-JSON stdout) throw SeedsCliError.

name: bootScheduler wraps spawnRun for in-process dispatch
description: src/server/scheduler.ts mirrors bootBridges shape: takes the server dep bag, wraps spawnRun in a DispatchSpawnFn that threads projectsConfig + projectSpawn + warrenConfigs (same plumbing as POST /runs handler), and calls bridges.start(runId, burrowRun.id) after a successful spawn so scheduled-run events flow into warren.events. The seam keeps a single source of truth for run dispatch — every code path that creates a run (UI/CLI POST, cron, scheduled-for) goes through the same spawnRun + bridges.start ritual.

content: bootServer teardown order: handle.stop() (HTTP listener) → scheduler.stop() → bridges.stopAll() → burrowClient.close() → closeDatabase. The scheduler must drain BEFORE bridges/burrow/db go away because an in-flight tick may be mid-spawnRun against burrow. The HTTP listener stops first so no new POSTs land mid-teardown while the scheduler is draining.

GET /projects/:id/triggers (warren-99c3) returns {triggers: TriggerSummary[], errors: WarrenConfigFileError[]} — TriggerSummary is the YAML entry flattened (id, kind, cron, seed, role, optional timezone/prompt) joined with persisted lastFiredAt/lastRunId from the triggers table plus a freshly-computed nextFireAt via croner. parseError is non-null when the cron passes warren-config's loose 5/6-token check but fails croner's strict parse; in that case nextFireAt falls back to the persisted row value rather than null. Built via src/triggers/summary.ts buildTriggerSummaries(). YAML schema/parse errors flow through verbatim from loadWarrenConfig.errors.

POST /projects/:id/triggers/:triggerId/run (warren-99c3) Run Now: resolves trigger from warren-config (404 if missing), spawnRun with trigger='manual-trigger' + metadata {triggerId, cron, seed}, bridges.start, then triggers.recordFire(now, parsedCron.nextRun(now), runId) when the cron parses (else upsert lastFiredAt+lastRunId only). Returns 201 {run, burrow: {id, workspacePath}} — same envelope as POST /runs. Manual fires update lastFiredAt so cron's prev>last semantic stays correct on the next tick.

Acceptance harness sets WARREN_SCHEDULER_TICK_MS=1000 globally (scripts/acceptance/run.ts extraEnv) so scenario 15's live cron + scheduledFor dispatch completes within budget. Other scenarios don't configure .warren/triggers.yaml or scheduledFor seeds, so the faster tick is a no-op for them (pl-2f15 risk #8 mitigation).

Scenario 15 must bootstrap .seeds/config.yaml in the project source repo before triggering the scheduler — sd list refuses to run without it. The fixture's .seeds/ ships only issues.jsonl, so any scenario shelling out to the seeds CLI needs to write config.yaml (project + version + max_plan_depth) first; this side-effect is fine to leave in place for sibling scenarios that read JSONL directly.

title: Use croner for cron parsing in src/triggers/
rationale: Cron grammar has enough corner cases (step values, ranges, day-of-week vs day-of-month interaction, 6-token seconds, timezone + DST) that a homegrown parser would either be wrong or grow into croner anyway. Croner is one small dep (~30 KB, tz-aware, actively maintained, no native deps); warren already has heavier deps (drizzle, zod, js-yaml). The 'tiny surface' argument in ROADMAP R-06 open-question #1 only held if we skipped tz + DST handling, which we explicitly want. Rejected alternatives: bring-our-own parser (wrong on edge cases or grows into croner), external cron daemon (re-introduces operator complexity, breaks SPEC §1 'one container' promise), routing cron through greenhouse (couples sibling repos for a one-process feature).

title: Scheduler does not catch up after warren downtime
rationale: Cron is 'fire at time T,' not 'fire N missed runs.' A 4-hour outage on an hourly trigger would dispatch 4 stacked runs and surprise operators (and any external system observing the run rate). ROADMAP R-06 open-question #4 leaned no; we follow standard cron semantics. Implementation: first observation of a fresh trigger seeds lastFiredAt=now and computes nextFireAt = parsedCron.nextRun(now) (mx-ac8acd), so a long outage skips silently. Operators who want manual replay press Run Now (POST /projects/:id/triggers/:triggerId/run). Future escape hatch — env var WARREN_SCHEDULER_CATCHUP=1 — is deferred to a follow-up if operator pressure shows up.

title: Write-once semantics: warren triggers row is the source of truth, not seeds extensions
rationale: When a scheduled seed fires, the write order is: spawn run FIRST → if spawn succeeds, write triggers.last_fired_at → THEN attempt sd update --extensions to clear scheduledFor + set lastScheduledRun. A failed sd-update is surfaced as a system event on the dispatched run, NOT a hard failure that re-fires the seed on the next tick. The warren row is authoritative (mx-a2ea60); the next tick reads it, sees the recent fire, and skips. This is the pl-2f15 risk #4 mitigation (seeds shell-out double-dispatch): if sd update --extensions failed and we treated it as authoritative, every tick would re-spawn the same seed. Symmetrically, cron dispatch writes the triggers row before counting the fire as 'done' so a crash mid-tick can't cause double-fire either (mx-eb4a3a single-flight guard prevents overlap within one process).

content: Seeds CLI shell-out (sd list / sd update --extensions) lives in src/seeds-cli/, not src/triggers/. The seeds-cli facade owns SeedsCliError, the SeedsListEnvelopeSchema/SeedRow/ScheduledSeed/parseScheduledSeeds parser, and the listScheduledSeeds + clearScheduledFor operations. triggers/tick.ts and triggers/dispatch.ts import ScheduledSeed from '../seeds-cli/index.ts'; server/scheduler.ts imports listScheduledSeeds + clearScheduledFor from there too. triggers/index.ts no longer re-exports those symbols — the R-01 producer side (post-dispatch updateExtensions) needs them outside the cron path, so the facade is shared, not trigger-private.
